title: Stable Merge Sort with Incorrect Swap

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a stable merge sort algorithm. The implementation should correctly handle edge cases and provide a stable sort. However, there is a bug in the merge process that causes the algorithm to produce incorrect results.
statement.inputFormat: The function takes an array of integers as input.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return an array of integers sorted in non-decreasing order.
statement.notes: The implementation should use a stable merge sort with a time complexity of O(n log n) and space complexity of O(n).

examples.explanation: Sorts the input array in non-decreasing order

functionSignature.cpp: void merge(vector<int>& nums, int left, int mid, int right);
functionSignature.java: void merge(int[] nums, int left, int mid, int right);
functionSignature.python: def merge(self, nums: list[int], left: int, mid: int, right: int)-> None:
